site.name: Atulya
site.colophon: Built with React, GSAP, Lenis. Type in Inter Tight & Instrument Serif.

hero.asideLede: Independent designer working between product, brand and interaction. Previously at studios you might know, now shipping small honest things from Copenhagen.

featured.heading:
A handful of projects I'm still
quietly proud of.

projects.title: Reinventing the Everyday Voucher
projects.summary: A unified loyalty wallet that turns daily errands into a quiet reward — rebuilt around one tappable card and a light visual grammar.
projects.contextLede: The team came in with fragmented tools and a long list of feature requests. We responded by doing less: a shared language, fewer primitives, and a quieter front door that the product could finally grow around.

quote.text: They reframed the problem before they touched the pixels — and the product got measurably calmer as a result.
quote.author: — Head of Product, Pointvoucher

process.v: Diary studies with 14 users over three weeks.
A single set of spacing and type tokens.
Collapsing four onboarding screens into two.
Micro-interactions, 90% at 60fps.

projects.outcomes: Activation up 41%, support tickets down 28%, and a team that finally trusts its own component library. The product got simpler; the people, less tired.

projects.title: Shaping a Voice Worth Hearing
projects.summary: A quiet editorial site for a composer — typography doing the heavy lifting, sound sitting comfortably beside the image.
projects.contextLede: Voice-first design for a classical composer. We set the type first, then asked what sound would earn a place on the same page.

quote.text: He gave me an internet that finally felt like mine — patient, legible, and quiet.
quote.author: — Rasmus Noah Hansen

process.v: Three conversations, one studio visit, two tea breaks.
Setting a voice on the page before touching layout.
Hover audio peeks; no autoplay, ever.
Removing two nav items and one hero video.

projects.outcomes: Bounce rate down, session time doubled, and a composer who is finally proud to share his own URL.

projects.title: Love, Endorsed by your Inner Circle
projects.summary: A dating concept built on trust — friends introduce friends. Warm, tactile, and deliberately slow.
projects.contextLede: We designed a dating app that made speed feel undignified. Everything in it is a trade: your attention for your friends' care.

quote.text: For the first time it felt like someone who knew me was doing the swiping.
quote.author: — Beta user, Copenhagen

process.v: Triangle model: you, a friend, a stranger they vouch for.
One introduction per day. Purposefully.
Warm typography, soft elevations, zero gamification.
Data stays between the three. No explore feed.

projects.outcomes: D30 retention 3.4× the dating app average; a quieter inbox that users opened voluntarily.

projects.title: Connecting Minds to Accelerate Innovation
projects.summary: A platform for founders and scientists — threading collaboration, research and funding into one coherent surface.
projects.contextLede: Three separate platforms were consolidated into one — without losing the specific rituals of each community.

quote.text: They built us a grammar we could argue in — and the arguments finally converged.
quote.author: — Platform Lead, BII

process.v: One week mapping overlaps across three products.
A typography and spacing scale both audiences agreed on.
Three home screens, one platform.
Contribution rules for a fifteen-person team.

projects.outcomes: Onboarding time halved, support tickets down by a third, and a shared language that survived the redesign.

projects.title: A Calmer Way to Move Your Money
projects.summary: A consumer banking app that replaces dashboards with one honest question: what are you trying to do today?
projects.contextLede: Banking apps default to data walls. We tried a softer front door — one that asked, and then answered.

quote.text: Our least-stressed users are now our most active ones.
quote.author: — CPO, Northbound

process.v: What are you trying to do today?
Dashboards tucked one tap away.
Transitions that confirm, not celebrate.
A palette that felt like linen, not a trading terminal.

projects.outcomes: NPS up 22, weekly active up 18%, support tone warmer. Not every bank needs more buttons.

projects.title: A Studio Identity, Woven in Paper
projects.summary: A small studio identity that refuses a mark — letters set in lead, type wandering between print and pixel.
projects.contextLede: A two-person studio with fifteen notebooks and no logo. We built an identity from the margins of those books.

quote.text: It felt like they found our identity, not invented it.
quote.author: — Co-founder, Field & Form

process.v: Two weeks of studio visits and notebook reading.
A single mono paired with a warm serif.
Identity lives off-screen as much as on.
A wordmark, not a symbol.

projects.outcomes: Two studios merged. One voice. Zero logos.

items.v: Tokens, components, governance; built to outlast me.
Figma, code, motion. Resolving arguments the fast way.
Type, image, pacing — tone before features.
GSAP, Lottie, Rive. Movement with a reason to move.

aboutTeaser.heading: I believe good interfaces are the ones you don't notice — the ones that move out of the way so the work can speak.
aboutTeaser.sub: Fifteen years in, I'm still drawn to the small moments: the hover that confirms, the transition that reassures, the line of type that carries a whole brand on its back.

archive.heading:
Everything else — fifty-six
projects, in plain text.
archive.sub: Shorter notes on shipped work. Hover a row to peek.

published.heading:
Essays, talks and the odd
argument with myself.

items.t: On slowness and the design of restraint
What we keep when we ship

bio: Hi — I'm Atulya. I design products and identities that try to do less, better. My work sits at the intersection of interaction, type and tone. I care about legibility, pacing, and the small sound a button makes when it's been considered.

I grew up between cities — Mumbai, Berlin, Copenhagen — and most of what I know I learned from the people around me. Before going independent I spent eight years in product studios, shipping across finance, health, media and the quiet kind of consumer apps you keep on your home screen.
Outside of client work I write essays, teach the occasional workshop, and keep a shelf of handmade notebooks that never quite get used.

footer.lead: Currently taking on two new projects for autumn — interaction-led product work, thoughtful brand systems, and long-form case studies.
